---
title: ""
output:
  html_document
  
---




```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE
)


#### Set Environment ####

library(usethis)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(htmltools) 
library(htmlwidgets)
library(gtExtras)
library(gt)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(sysfonts)
library(showtext)

font_add_google("Roboto", "roboto")
font_add_google("Archivo", "archivo")

showtext_auto()  # tells ggplot to use showtext for rendering
showtext_opts(dpi = 300)

### See current RACE COUNTS style guide: W:\Project\RACE COUNTS\Style Guide\RC-Style Guide.pdf and
####### RC_Website_Fonts_Color_Ramp_2023.txt

#Add database connection and source script with functions for pushing to postgres
source("W:\\RDA Team\\R\\credentials_source.R")
options(scipen = 999)

```


```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE
)

#### Pull in data ####
source("./calcs_hateincident.R")
df <- final_df 

# need code below to rename within the chart fx rather than in the source data
df <- df %>%
  # clean up labels for visuals
  mutate(race_label=case_when(
    race=="latino" ~ "Latinx",
    race=="aian" ~ "AIAN",
    race=="nh_black" ~ "Black",
    race=="nhpi" ~ "PI",
    race=="swana" ~ "SWANA",
    race=="nh_white" ~ "White",
    race=="nh_asian" ~ 'Asian',
    race=="total" ~ 'Total',
      TRUE~NA))

```

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE
)

## COLORS ## source: RC_Website_Fonts_Color_Ramp_2023.txt
  my_colors = c("#FF6B02",  # orange, core RC color
                "#F91105",  # red, core RC color
                "#AC0068",  # purple, core RC color
                "#FEC009",  # yellow, core RC color
                "#0077B6",  # sapphire blue
                "#008B5E",  # emerald green
                "#5C2D91",   # amethyst
                "#00868A"   # deep teal
             )
  
  rc_white = "#F7F7F7"     # rc white


```

## People who have Experienced Hate Incidents


```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
############# STATIC VERSION ############# 

# calculate width based on longest label
max_label_width <- max(nchar(paste0(round(df$rate, 1), "% (", scales::comma(df$raw), ")")))
chart_width <- max(7, max_label_width * 0.15)

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = rate, y = reorder(race_label, rate), fill = race)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(rate, 1), "% (", scales::comma(raw), ")")),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Between 2023-2024, nearly 3M Californians experienced one or more hate incidents",
    x = "Percent of People Who Have Experienced a Hate Incident in the Last 12 Months (#)",
    y = NULL,
    caption = 'Data Source: California Health Interview Survey 2023-24 Pooled Estimates. Teens and adults were asked "During the\npast 12 months, do you think you directly experienced a hate incident?"\n\nData Notes: AIAN = American Indian / Alaska Native, PI = Pacific Islander, SWANA = Southwest Asian / North African.\nThose three races include people who identify as that race alone or in combination with another race or ethnicity including\nLatinx. Asian, Black and White races include people who identify as that race alone.'
    ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "roboto") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 7)),  # t = top margin, in points
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),     # remove x-axis labels
    plot.title.position = "plot",   # set alignment based on whole image, not chart
    plot.title = element_text(hjust=.5, family = "archivo"),
    plot.caption.position = "plot", # set alignment based on whole image, not chart
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 15)),  # left align, add space from x-axis label
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),  # top, right, bottom, left in points
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p

ggplot2::ggsave("hate_incident_race.png",
       plot = p,
       width = 7,
       height = 4.5,
       units = "in",
       dpi = 300,
       bg = rc_white)

```
